title: Schertz
name: Schertz
metaDescription: 1031 exchange coordination for Schertz industrial, retail, and multifamily property near JBSA-Randolph, with tri-county documentation handled before your window runs.

sections.html: Schertz sits at the intersection of I-35 and FM 3009 in the tri-county area shared by Bexar, Guadalupe, and Comal, immediately adjacent to Joint Base San Antonio-Randolph. Investors identifying replacement property here are usually trading into industrial, flex, or retail assets tied to distribution growth and the base's civilian workforce, and the documentation load looks different from a single-county file because permitting and tax records can span three jurisdictions.

sections.heading: Schertz Asset Stock Near JBSA-Randolph
sections.html:
Industrial and flex space along the rail line and I-35 frontage has expanded to serve regional distribution, and several last-mile logistics operators have leased space here rather than in tighter San Antonio submarkets. Retail has followed the base's civilian and military workforce along FM 3009 and Main Street, and medical office has picked up leasing tied to the population supporting Randolph.

Multifamily product serves both base personnel and the broader Schertz-Cibolo-Universal City growth corridor, and each of these classes is like-kind eligible against whatever real property an investor is exiting elsewhere.

Big-box and grocery-anchored retail along FM 3009 has also picked up leasing activity as the residential base along the corridor has expanded, giving an exchange buyer several distinct sub-corridors to compare rather than a single retail strip. Rail service through parts of Schertz supports a narrower set of industrial users who specifically need siding access, which sets those buildings apart from purely truck-served flex product elsewhere in the metro.

sections.heading: Demand Drivers in the Golden Triangle
sections.html:
Schertz, Cibolo, and Universal City are often discussed together as a single growth corridor, and Schertz's industrial base benefits from that shared rooftop growth even where the parcel itself sits closer to the rail line than to any one city center. JBSA-Randolph provides a stable base of employment that supports retail and multifamily demand independent of broader San Antonio market cycles.

No state income tax continues to be cited by relocating logistics and light-manufacturing tenants weighing Schertz against out-of-state alternatives. Lease renewal activity among existing Schertz industrial tenants has also stayed steady, which is a sign that occupiers value the site's dual access to I-35 and the regional rail network rather than treating it as a temporary location.

sections.heading: Multi-County Closing Coordination for Schertz Property
sections.html:
Because Schertz spans Bexar, Guadalupe, and Comal county lines depending on the parcel, identification and closing coordination has to confirm which county's records and permitting apply before the 45-day window is used up. Typical documentation items include:

  • County-specific tax certificate and plat confirmation for the parcel's actual jurisdiction
  • Written identification meeting the unambiguous-description standard under Treasury Regulation 1.1031(k)-1
  • Rail-access or crossing agreements where the asset depends on siding access
  • Lender preflight to confirm debt replacement and avoid boot
  • Exchange agreement and assignment paperwork routed to the qualified intermediary ahead of the closing date

This is documentation and process coordination only; tax positioning is the investor's advisor's call.

sections.heading: Watchouts for Schertz Industrial and Retail
sections.html:
Utility capacity, particularly water and wastewater service, has lagged rooftop and industrial growth in parts of Schertz, and a buyer should confirm service commitments before relying on a pro forma build-out timeline. Rail-crossing access can also affect a site's usability for certain industrial tenants, and that detail is easy to miss in a fast-moving identification.

Because Schertz competes with Cibolo and Universal City for the same tenant pool, cap rate compression on stabilized retail has been more pronounced here than in less contested Bexar County submarkets.

An investor comparing a Schertz property against one in Cibolo or Universal City should pull separate utility and permitting confirmations for each parcel, since a shared corridor name does not mean shared infrastructure capacity or governing jurisdiction.

sections.heading: Coordinating With a Qualified Intermediary on Schertz Property
sections.html: The qualified intermediary's role in a Schertz file is the same as anywhere else in the metro: hold proceeds, prepare exchange documents, and keep the transaction outside of constructive receipt. Where a Schertz parcel spans a county line or depends on rail access that takes longer to confirm than expected, a reverse exchange structure is sometimes worth discussing with your QI and tax advisor before the 45-day window closes on a single option.

faqs.q: Does a Schertz industrial building qualify as like-kind if I'm exchanging out of retail?
faqs.a: Yes, both are real property held for business or investment use, which satisfies the like-kind requirement regardless of asset class. Your tax advisor should still confirm your specific exchange facts.

faqs.q: Which county's records apply to a Schertz property that sits near a jurisdictional line?
faqs.a: It depends on the parcel; Schertz extends into Bexar, Guadalupe, and Comal counties, so tax certificates and plat records should be pulled from the county that actually has jurisdiction over that specific site before you rely on them for identification.

faqs.q: Can I still hit my 45-day window if a Schertz property needs a rail-access confirmation first?
faqs.a: The window doesn't extend for due diligence delays, so rail-access and utility-capacity confirmations should happen before you commit the property to your written identification, not after.

faqs.q: Is a reverse exchange ever used for Schertz property?
faqs.a: Yes, reverse exchange coordination is an option when a Schertz replacement property needs to close before the relinquished property sells, though it requires an exchange accommodation titleholder and added documentation compared to a forward exchange.

faqs.q: Who confirms utility capacity for a Schertz industrial site before closing?
faqs.a: That confirmation comes from the relevant utility provider and the investor's own engineering or due-diligence team; coordination on our end tracks the documentation and timeline, not the engineering review itself.
